Marriage in Trouble.
Can this couple be saved?
Next, on a very special Dr.Steve Show
[Transcript from episode #42]
Welcome to the Dr. Steve show. 
We’ve got a story today that’5 centered around the top two reasons why 
couples split up—finances and sleep.
Today’5 troubled pair, Ryan and Monica, share a bed and a 
bank account. But not for long if we can’t find a solution. The 
problem? The classic “two people—one bank account” thing. 
Here’5 how Monica described it to me:
“Ryan and 1 agreed that neither of us will overdraw the checking account. 
50 the procedure is, whoever wants to withdraw money must check the 
balance in the account before making the withdrawal. It all seemed 50 
simple. But suddenly we’re bouncing checks and getting hit with overdraft 
fees!
1 thought it wasn’t possible, 1 thought our procedure was safe. But then 
this happened:
Ryan needed $50, 50 he checked the balance in the account, 
and 
saw that it was $100. No problem. 50, he plans to withdraw 
the money. But first he falls asleep! 
And that’5 where 1 come in, while Ryan’5 still asleep, 
and now 1 want to withdraw $100. 1 check the balance, 
and it’5 $100 (because Ryan’5 still asleep and hasn’t 
yet 
made his withdrawal), 50 1 think, no problem. 50 1 make 
the withdrawal, and again no problem. But then Ryan wakes up, 
completes his withdrawal, and we’re suddenly overdrawn! He didn’t 
even know that he fell asleep, 50 he just went ahead and completed his 
transaction without checking the balance again. You’ve got to help us Dr. 
Steve!”
15 there a solution? Are they doomed? We can’t stop Ryan from falling 
asleep, but can we make sure that Monica can’t get her hands on the bank 
account until after he wakes up? 
Take a moment and think about that while we go to a commercial break.
Ryan falls asleep after 
he checks the balance 
but before he makes the 
withdrawal. When he wakes 
up, he immediately makes 
the withdrawl without 
checking the balance again.
Ryan and Monica: victims 
of the “two people, one 
account” problem.
